steps.title: Identify the temporal claim
steps.content: The key signal is the researchers' statement that “invasive” and “native” are labels describing temporary circumstances, explained by climate change shifting species ranges and creating new habitats.

steps.title: Apply to Canada's classification
steps.content:
If these labels are temporary (as the researchers show), then Canada's designation of the round goby as invasive is also temporary — appropriate now but potentially obsolete in the future as climate reshapes available habitats.

The other choices either reference an unmentioned past, suggest the distinction itself is invalid (which the passage doesn't argue), or contradict the climate-change logic.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
